title: Patent
content:
- There's a prior art paper [Generative Model based Highly Efficient Semantic Communication Approach for Image Transmission | IEEE Conference Publication | IEEE Xplore](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10096372)
- Rework it specifically for autonomous undersea surveys. [[Could]] SCHEDULED: <2024-01-31 Wed>
- Transfer ownership to Andrew Melchior
- Based on the information provided and a search through recent research papers, I have evaluated and ranked the papers in terms of their suitability for encoding multiple streams of image data as latent space embeddings for transmission over low bitrate channels. Here's the ranking and a brief description of each paper:
- collapsed:: true
  - Score: 9/10
  - Progressive deep image compression framework suitable for wireless communications, showing superior performance in classification, distortion, and perception metrics.
  - [(Lei et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/deep-image-compression-hybrid-contexts-image-lei/f6ee08c02697583dbae32d7d7492de1a/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- collapsed:: true
  - Score: 8.5/10
  - Introduces task-generic image compression using end-to-end learned image compression, focusing on quality metrics in the latent space.
  - [(Gao et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/towards-taskgeneric-image-compression-study-gao/99b6d01831c955d0bd886c0c47edb06b/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- collapsed:: true
  - Score: 8/10
  - Focuses on hyperspectral image compression via implicit neural representations, achieving better compression at low bitrates.
  - [(Rezasoltani & Qureshi, 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/image-compression-using-implicit-neural-representation-rezasoltani/9d1cd3206bea573b82f0b626061e020c/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- collapsed:: true
  - Score: 7.5/10
  - Proposes edge-guided hyperspectral compression with an interactive dual attention module, enhancing structural representation at low bit rates.
  - [(Guo et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/hyperspectral-image-compression-with-interactive-dual-guo/b0509279f5a255a8b5096df9bd19cefe/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- collapsed:: true
  - Score: 7/10
  - Discusses learned image compression methods with multistage spatial context models, offering fast decoding and improved rate-distortion performance.
  - [(Lin et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/multistage-spatial-context-models-learned-image-lin/12ce588c33f553c595179ea715a09847/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- collapsed:: true
  - Score: 6.5/10
  - Introduces a method using super-resolution residual learning network to improve the quality of compressed images.
  - [(Hu et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/image-compression-method-using-image-superresolution-hu/16e01b6073d35d0e8d2c89a6115c8b99/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- **Raw Image Reconstruction with Learned Compact Metadata** (Wang et al., 2023)
  - Score: 6/10
  - Proposes a novel framework for raw image compression with learned compact metadata in the latent space.
  - [(Wang et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/image-reconstruction-learned-compact-metadata-wang/f8b86fd89dda589d85f1702466e2d930/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- **Quantum Approach to Image Data Encoding and Compression** (Majji et al., 2023)
  - Score: 5.5/10
  - Presents a novel approach to compress images using quantum computing, offering exponential scaling.
  - [(Majji et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/quantum-approach-image-data-encoding-compression-majji/354e4e14b25152298d483b8f505adf6f/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- **Improving Statistical Fidelity for Neural Image Compression with Implicit Local Likelihood Models** (Muckley et al., 2023)
  - Score: 5/10
  - Discusses improving statistical fidelity in lossy image compression using non-binary discriminators.
  - [(Muckley et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/improving-fidelity-neural-image-compression-implicit-muckley/7857868625d4519691f8c37d5f9a8800/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- **Modality-Agnostic Variational Compression of Implicit Neural Representations** (Schwarz et al., 2023)
  - Score: 4.5/10
  - Introduces a modality-agnostic neural compression algorithm based on implicit neural representations.
  - [(Schwarz et al., 2023)](https://consensus.app/papers/variational-compression-implicit-neural-schwarz/a5ba31b988ef5d0786dc778dd6bafd03/?utm_source=chatgpt)
- raw
- 1. Task-Generic Image Compression Using Semantics-Oriented Metrics (Gao et al., 2023) Gao et al. introduced a novel image compression method focusing on semantics. The method uses deep feature distance in latent space aligned with image semantics. An importance-weighted pixel distance metric reflects semantic importance in pixels. The combined metric achieved significant savings in bits and enhanced visual quality. This approach marks a substantial step in task-generic image compression techniques. BibTeX Citation mathematica Copy code @article{gao2023task, title={Task-Generic Image Compression Using Semantics-Oriented Metrics}, author={Gao, [First Names]}, journal={Journal Name}, volume={Vol. Number}, pages={Page Range}, year={2023} }
- GPT Talked to chat.consensus.app Based on your interest in papers about compression for transmission where AI latent space is the compression medium, I found several relevant papers published after 2023. Here are some that align with your criteria:
- Towards Task-Generic Image Compression: A Study of Semantics-Oriented Metrics by Gao, Liu, Li, & Wu (2023) explores task-generic image compression using end-to-end learned image compression and focuses on distortion metrics that estimate the quality of reconstructed images. It demonstrates significant bits saving and improved visual quality (Gao, Liu, Li, & Wu, 2023).
- Progressive Deep Image Compression for Hybrid Contexts of Image Classification and Reconstruction by Lei et al. (2023) introduces a framework for progressive deep image compression suitable for wireless communications, multi-user broadcasting, and multi-tasking applications. It shows superior performance in classification, distortion, and perception metrics (Lei et al., 2023).
- Photonic Unsupervised Learning Variational Autoencoder for High-Throughput and Low-Latency Image Transmission by Chen et al. (2023) presents an all-optical variational autoencoder for high-throughput processing in image transmission systems, significantly reducing system latency and transmission error ratio (Chen et al., 2023).
- Compressing Tabular Data via Latent Variable Estimation by Montanari & Weiner (2023) introduces a family of lossless compression algorithms for data tables with categorical entries. It emphasizes the efficiency and effectiveness of these data compression algorithms in various applications (Montanari & Weiner, 2023).
- Multistage Spatial Context Models for Learned Image Compression by Lin et al. (2023) focuses on learned image compression methods featuring spatial context models, offering fast decoding and improved rate-distortion performance (Lin et al., 2023).
